transfer_mechanism: fit more county annual threshold variants until one looks stable
caller_evidence: 2026-04-22 critique-close confirmed threshold diffuseness and unresolved annual causal identification
notes: Baseline attractor basin, not a real survivor

transfer_mechanism: model backlog, overflow, and waiting-time failure at shelter, school, hospital, and court nodes
caller_evidence: Receiver-city shelter saturation was the clearest crisis case while county wage causality stayed unresolved
notes: Best direct escape from average county incidence

transfer_mechanism: measure load against institution-specific capacity rather than generic county population
caller_evidence: Boston and Chicago imposed large costs without the same shelter ratio break, implying institution-specific constraints
notes: Separates housing, shelter, schools, courts, clinics

transfer_mechanism: re-weight outcomes by incumbent resident exposure and split renter, homeowner, low-skill, parent, taxpayer incidence
caller_evidence: 2026-04-22 critique-close confirmed median-county newcomer ratios misstate typical resident exposure
notes: Needed to answer the actual destination-country question

transfer_mechanism: treat county flow-capacity as a risk score for overload rather than a causal threshold parameter
caller_evidence: Threshold surface stayed diffuse even when wage-search performance beat the null
notes: Matches the actual evidence shape better than breakpoint rhetoric

transfer_mechanism: replace county averages with neighborhood or district concentration measures around shelters, schools, and rental markets
caller_evidence: County averages likely dilute local overload, but public micro-geo data is incomplete in the current repo
notes: Promising, but data-heavy and likely downstream of receiver-node work

transfer_mechanism: split incidence by pathway, assignment regime, and legal-service obligations before estimating any common effect
caller_evidence: Right-to-shelter and emergency assignment regimes already looked first-order in receiver-city memos
notes: Moves composition and governance from nuisance controls to core causes

transfer_mechanism: define outcomes as failure events such as shelter overflow, hotel spillover, class-size jumps, court backlog, and emergency procurement
caller_evidence: The strongest observed harm channels were service failures, not broad county employment collapse

transfer_mechanism: measure cumulative weeks or months above local capacity instead of single-period load ratios
caller_evidence: A diffuse threshold surface suggests persistence may matter more than a single crossing point
notes: More plausible mechanism for degradation and backlash

transfer_mechanism: use quasi-exogenous routing, busing, hotel contracting, or court-placement shocks rather than ACS entered-stock proxies
caller_evidence: Current recent-flow proxy is timing-blurred and the strongest clean cases are concentrated assignment regimes
notes: Potentially the cleanest causal design if local admin traces can be assembled

dominant_paradigm_escaped: immigrant voting as main political channel
transfer_mechanism: model backlash as a response to visible overload and legitimacy failure, not mainly to immigrant ideology
caller_evidence: Caplan audit and county work both indicated load-capacity stress fit politics better than immigrant-voter stories

transfer_mechanism: treat housing, shelter, schooling, courts, and labor as separate failure modes with different breakpoints
caller_evidence: Repeated reviews showed outcome-specific ambiguity and no single threshold generalized well across outcomes
notes: Most compatible with the observed heterogeneity

transfer_mechanism: treat housing permits, shelter beds, court slots, and school seats as scarce slots whose queue spillovers generate downstream delays and diversions
caller_evidence: Overflow and reassignment effects already appear more concrete than average wage effects

transfer_mechanism: focus on tail-risk reserve depletion and emergency outlays rather than average annual balances
caller_evidence: Local fiscal burdens and emergency shelter procurement look like reserve stress, not smooth average-cost absorption
notes: Good fit for state-local budget incidence

transfer_mechanism: treat metro and district nodes as heterogeneous habitat patches with different carrying capacity and spillover routes
caller_evidence: Conceptually useful, but it risks becoming metaphor without stronger node-level data
notes: Useful as a framing aid, not yet a first implementation target

transfer_mechanism: bound how much destination-capacity decay would be required to erase migrant place-premium gains
caller_evidence: Open-borders audit now hinges on whether capacity erosion stays subcritical
notes: Could settle the strongest slogan dispute with bounded math rather than more stylized modeling

dominant_paradigm_escaped: full causal graph before saying anything
transfer_mechanism: state cheap necessary conditions that must hold for optimistic or pessimistic stories to survive, then kill the ones that fail
caller_evidence: This repo already improved whenever critique forced explicit kill criteria instead of vague plausibility claims

dominant_paradigm_escaped: median county as unit of welfare
transfer_mechanism: replace county-median summaries with resident-weighted, renter-weighted, and student-weighted exposure metrics
caller_evidence: 2026-04-22 critique-close confirmed the median-county newcomer comparison was the wrong exposure summary
notes: Fastest repair to one live measurement problem

transfer_mechanism: formalize every claim as migrant/global, national aggregate, resident per-capita, or local incumbent before any evidence update
caller_evidence: Ledger confusion repeatedly caused answer drift, but this is governance more than new empirical leverage
notes: Useful discipline, not the main empirical frontier
